visual_description: A medium shot captures a somber scene in what appears to be a morgue or a medical examination room. The room has light-colored walls and wooden doors in the background. In the center of the frame, a man in a light-colored, long-sleeved shirt and dark pants carefully maneuvers a gurney, upon which a pale, discolored body lies. The body is covered in what looks like bruises or discoloration. To the left, another man in a white shirt and tie observes the scene. Standing behind the gurney is a man in a dark suit and tie, looking down at the body with a serious expression. The camera remains steady, focusing on the group's interaction with the body. A man speaks in a calm, professional, and inquisitive tone, asking, "What do you make of these?" Another man replies in a similarly professional but slightly more analytical voice, "Different configuration than the other victims." The first man then directs, "Get a close-up," his voice remaining steady and focused. A woman with short brown hair, wearing a dark red top and a dark skirt, enters the frame from the left and approaches the gurney to get a closer look. The man in the suit remains in the background
